message:
fix(raptorq): dynamic symbol pool sizing and bench transport capacity

Two related fixes that prevent RaptorQ pipeline failures when encoding
objects whose source+repair symbol count exceeds the configured pool
hint, plus release/bench profile optimization.

pipeline.rs:
- SymbolPool max_size is now dynamically computed as the maximum of the
  configured pool_size hint and the actual number of source + repair
  symbols needed for the current object. Previously, max_size was set
  to the static config hint, which caused pool exhaustion and silent
  failures when encoding large objects with high repair overhead.
- The computation uses div_ceil for source_count and adds repair_count
  to get the true needed_symbols.

benches/phase0_baseline.rs:
- Transport capacity (sim_channel buffer size) is now computed based on
  the actual number of source + repair symbols with 25% headroom,
  instead of using the default capacity. The benchmark sends all symbols
  before the receiver reads, so the buffer must hold the full burst.
- Adds a comment explaining why the capacity sizing is necessary.

Cargo.toml:
- Add [profile.release]: opt-level 3, fat LTO, codegen-units 1, strip
  symbols — maximizes binary performance and minimizes size for release
  builds.
- Add [profile.bench]: opt-level 3, thin LTO, codegen-units 1 — ensures
  benchmarks run with representative optimization without the full cost
  of fat LTO link times.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -246,6 +246,17 @@ harness = false
 name = "methodology_baselines"
 harness = false
 
+[profile.release]
+opt-level = 3
+lto = "fat"
+codegen-units = 1
+strip = "symbols"
+
+[profile.bench]
+opt-level = 3
+lto = "thin"
+codegen-units = 1
+
 [lints.rust]
 # Default to deny for unsafe code - specific modules that require it can use #[allow(unsafe_code)]
 # The epoll reactor requires unsafe for the polling crate's FFI operations
